differential_diagnoses: Type B insulin resistance syndrome (insulin receptor autoantibodies)
Autoimmune lipodystrophy associated with systemic lupus erythematosus or other autoimmune disease
Primary insulin receptoropathy (e.g., Rabson-Mendenhall syndrome variant)
next_steps: Test for insulin receptor autoantibodies (Type B insulin resistance evaluation)
Perform comprehensive metabolic assessment including fasting serum insulin, C-peptide, and IGF-1
Evaluate for systemic autoimmune disease (e.g., anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, complement levels)
Initiate referral to a specialized metabolism/endocrinology center for possible leptin replacement therapy or specialized immunosuppression
Establish a consistent high-dose insulin regimen and avoid unnecessary invasive GI procedures
Monitor for complications of severe insulin resistance including hepatic, renal, and cardiac manifestations
